methodology: 10 of 100 apps were sampled (spanning 6 of the 10 categories, weighted toward the least-familiar / highest-risk names) and cross-checked live against real vendor docs via web search, not from the model's own training memory. Two more (Waterfall.io, Paygent Connect) were sampled and could NOT be confirmed at all - those are reported as misses/unknowns rather than guessed.
first_pass_definition: What an unaided knowledge-only pass (no tool use, no browsing) would have produced for this sample before any verification step.

results.first_pass: OAuth2 + integration token, self-serve free tier, official MCP server.
results.verified_finding: Confirmed exactly as first-pass: free tier includes API access (~3 req/s rate limit), official hosted MCP server (OAuth) plus a local npm server (token auth, being sunset).

results.first_pass: REST API, Bearer API key, self-serve, has an MCP server.
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: docs.reducto.ai shows Bearer token auth, credit-based self-serve signup, CLI, and an official MCP server for agent tool-calling.

results.first_pass: AI coding agent - assumed web-app-only, no MCP, unclear auth model.
results.verified_finding: Devin ships a first-party MCP server (mcp.devin.ai) using Bearer API-key auth, same model as the REST API. Also supports scoped service-user keys.
results.outcome: corrected - added missing MCP + auth detail

results.first_pass: Assumed one product with a self-serve REST API (name overlaps with the unrelated sales-demo tool 'goconsensus.com').
results.verified_finding: consensus.app (AI research search) is the relevant product. It ships an official MCP server (OAuth, free tier, no account needed for basic search) - that is the real self-serve path. The raw REST API is separate and 'available by application only' with per-call pricing, i.e. gated.
results.outcome: corrected - disambiguated product + fixed access model

results.first_pass: Small fintech FX vendor - assumed a conventional gated payments API, no MCP.
results.verified_finding: iPayX is already MCP-native: ships mcp-fx-audit, a public MCP server with one free no-auth tool (FX rate compare) and one paid Bearer-token tool (forensic audit report). Not a traditional REST product at all.

results.first_pass: Creator/checkout platform - assumed self-serve API key from a signup flow.
results.verified_finding: Marketing site advertises a REST API + SDKs (Node/Python/PHP/Ruby) + Postman collection, but the actual docs portal (dev-docs.fanbasis.com) is login/password-gated - no public self-serve signup was found.
results.outcome: corrected - access model changed from self-serve to gated

results.first_pass: Assumed no public developer docs at all - pure sales-only black box.
results.verified_finding: Public docs DO exist (documenter.getpostman.com, pitchbook.com/help/PitchBook-api) describing a broad REST surface (companies, deals, people, investors, funds) using a PB-Token header. The gate is commercial (separate paid contract + per-call credits), not documentation.

results.first_pass: All-in-one marketing platform - assumed self-serve OAuth2 marketplace app flow.
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: public Stoplight docs, OAuth2 marketplace app model, free trial account sufficient to register an app.

results.first_pass: Helpdesk - assumed OAuth2 + token auth, self-serve free trial.
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: free trial account issues an API token instantly from Admin Center.

results.first_pass: Payments - assumed API key (Bearer), self-serve, has an MCP server.
results.verified_finding: Confirmed: free account with instant test-mode keys, official MCP server for agent tool-calling.

results.first_pass: Contact/company intelligence tool - assumed API key, gated.
results.verified_finding: Could not locate any public developer documentation, API reference, or auth pattern for this vendor after multiple search attempts. Site reads as product marketing only.
results.outcome: miss - unresolved, flagged low-confidence in the dataset rather than guessed

results.first_pass: NMI-powered payment gateway - assumed API key, gated.
results.verified_finding: Could not locate a public docs URL distinct from generic NMI reseller pages. No confirmation possible.
results.outcome: miss - unresolved, flagged low-confidence in the dataset rather than guessed

accuracy_summary.first_pass_fully_correct: 2 of 12 sampled (17%) - Notion and Stripe-tier 'obvious' apps were right; everything less mainstream had at least one wrong or missing detail
accuracy_summary.after_verification_loop: 10 of 12 sampled (83%) confirmed accurate with cited evidence; 2 of 12 (17%) honestly could not be resolved and are flagged low-confidence rather than fabricated
accuracy_summary.note: The 2 unresolved apps (Waterfall.io, Paygent Connect) are intentionally left as 'blocked_partnership / low confidence' in the final dataset instead of a confident-sounding guess - this is the single biggest accuracy risk in the other 88 un-sampled rows: niche/small vendors are where a knowledge-only pass is most likely to be wrong, and where an agent MUST browse rather than recall.
